title: The Frontier of Ancient History
body:
Ancient DNA is changing assumptions about migration, ancestry, and population mixing. Satellite archaeology is locating sites that once looked invisible from the ground.

Digital imaging and text recovery are improving the reading of damaged inscriptions and manuscripts. That means new voices and new administrative details can emerge from old fragments.

The frontier also involves decolonising the ancient world, widening the field beyond a narrow Greco-Roman centre, and taking non-Western ancient civilisations seriously on their own terms.

Ancient history is becoming more data-rich while also becoming more globally honest.
keyPoints: Ancient DNA is changing migration stories
Non-Western ancient worlds are being centred more fairly
Old evidence is being read with new methods
